rationale: The supervisor uses systematic wall-following and rotation to avoid oscillations, ensures safe obstacle avoidance, and encourages full area coverage by rotating and changing direction after encountering obstacles. Helper states (e.g., 'turning', 'wall_follow') are introduced to manage transitions between obstacle responses and resuming exploration.
coverage_strategy: Use wall-following in the presence of side obstacles to ensure systematic exploration along boundaries.
On encountering a frontal obstacle, rotate in place (direction chosen based on side obstacles) to find a new clear path.
When in a clear state, move forward to maximize area coverage.
Periodically perform a full rotation to detect new paths and avoid missing unexplored regions.
Transition out of wall-following when the path is clear to resume open exploration.
Avoid oscillations by having explicit turning and wall-following modes.
